description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the default alpine Docker image (and alpine:3.10 for the generate_pages job) with three stages: test, generate, and deploy. In the test stage define black, flake8, mypy, pylint, and tests jobs; each runs "apk update && apk add bash python3 python3-dev py-pip" (black and mypy also add "apk add build-base"; pylint adds "apk add --update alpine-sdk"; tests add "apk add build-base linux-headers"), then installs their Python tools with pip3 (black installs black==22.12.0, flake8 installs flake8, mypy installs mypy types-requests, pylint installs pylint decorator requests, tests install pytest requests psutil pytest-mock) and finally executes the corresponding script (./ci_scripts/black.sh, ./ci_scripts/flake8.sh, mypy tes_lib, ./ci_scripts/pylint.sh, ./ci_scripts/run_tests.sh). The flake8, pylint, and generate_pages jobs each publish artifacts that expire after one day: flake8_issue_count.txt, pylint_score.txt, and the generated HTML and badge SVG files, and they are always uploaded. The generate_pages job runs in the generate stage using alpine:3.10, depends on pylint and flake8, installs additional packages with "apk add bash make python3 python3-dev py-pip py-gtk-dev git", installs sphinx and anybadge via pip3, and runs ./ci_scripts/generate_web_pages.sh and ./ci_scripts/generate_badges.sh. The deploy stage contains a pages job that runs only on the main branch, depends on generate_pages, creates a public directory, copies the built docs and badge SVGs into it, and publishes the public/ folder as GitLab Pages.
